preferences.text: The user wants warm, concise Chinese collaboration with concrete progress rather than abstract theory.
preferences.evidence: The user is exploring product direction interactively and asked whether the idea fits the skill format.

avoid.text: Do not treat full transcripts as memory; prioritize curated operating state.
avoid.evidence: Core problem stated by the user: copied conversation history does not tell a new model what matters.

project.name: Agent Memory Skill
project.objective: Create an open-source AI agent memory skill that preserves user preferences, project decisions, topic state, side ideas, and migration context across model or architecture changes.

facts.text: The first version is implemented as a Codex-compatible skill while keeping the memory protocol runtime-agnostic.
facts.evidence: Project structure contains agent-memory/SKILL.md plus adapter references.

artifacts.text: agent-memory/SKILL.md defines the core operating loop, salience gate, topic stack, and migration packet workflow.

active.text: Prepare the agent-memory skill as a GitHub-ready open-source prototype.
active.evidence: User said they plan to publish it on GitHub later.

parked.text: Clarify whether memory should be a skill, a library, or both.
parked.evidence: User asked whether this really counts as a skill.

decisions.text: Use one universal memory kernel with runtime-specific adapters instead of separate agent and chat memory projects.
decisions.evidence: This preserves shared memory semantics while allowing Codex, chat, autonomous agent, and multi-agent integrations.

episodes.text: The user raised a concern that most skills do practical external tasks, while this memory skill changes agent behavior. This should be framed as a meta-skill or continuity skill.
episodes.evidence: User explicitly asked whether this counts as a skill format.

migration.summary: Agent Memory Skill is a prototype continuity skill for AI agents. It should help future agents identify important context, preserve user preferences, manage topic interruptions, and create handoff packets for model or architecture migration.
next_actions: Expand tests for edge cases such as stale records, superseded decisions, and conflicting topic states.
Create a short demo showing a side topic being captured and the main thread resumed.
Consider extracting the script into a core package after the skill protocol stabilizes.
risks: If memory records become too verbose, the system will recreate transcript dumping in another form.
If adapters are mixed into the core protocol too early, the project may become Codex-specific.
handoff_notes: Keep the skill itself lean. Put GitHub-facing documentation outside the skill folder.
Treat inferred user preferences as tentative unless explicitly confirmed.
